title: Query Name
documentation:
## Purpose

A Query Name identifies a named query within a machine or cap, allowing it to be referenced by other components. It must consist only of alphanumeric characters, hyphens, and underscores, with a maximum length of 128 characters.

## When to use

Use this media type when passing a query identifier as an input to a node that dispatches or looks up a named query. It is commonly used alongside [Substitutions](https://capdag.com/browse/media:substitutions;textable;record) to parameterize the resolved query.

## Notes

The pattern constraint (`^[a-zA-Z0-9_-]+$`) ensures query names are safe for use as identifiers in URLs, file names, and configuration keys without escaping.
